semanticCore: Leaving alone, farewell, bidding adieu, deposit, quietness, or soft
quranicRelevance: God not abandoning the Prophet (Wadda'a - 'ma wadda'aka rabbuka'), and leaving/ignoring harm ('wada'').

keyDerivations.meaning: To leave alone, abandon, bid farewell, or desert

figurativeOrRareUsages: مَا وَدَّعَكَ رَبُّكَ وَمَا قَلَىٰ (93:3): Your Lord has not abandoned/deserted you, nor has He detested you (comforting Duha context).
discardedNoise: Depositing money (Wadee'ah) in modern savings banks.
Quiet meekness (Wada'ah) in character development profiles.
summaryEn: The root denotes leaving alone (Tawdee'), assuring the Prophet of divine guardianship (no abandonment) and instructing him to ignore hypocrites' harm.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الترك والتوديع، واصفاً نفي تخلي وترك الرب لنبيه الكريم وقت انقطاع الوحي (ما ودعك)، وأمره بالإعراض عن أذى الكفار.
rawText: إِنْوَدَعَ 7 see 1. اِتَّدَعَ 8 : see 1: he acted, or proceeded, with moderation, without haste or hurry, in his pace or journeying. (M in art. اون.) اِسْتَوْدَعَهُ مَالًا 10 He intrusted him with property; intrusted to him property; gave property to him in trust, or as a deposit. (Msb.) And اِسْتَوْدَعَهُ وَدِيعَةً He asked him to keep, preserve, guard, or take care of, a deposit. (K.) تَوَادَعَا 6 They two made peace, or became reconciled, each with the other. (K.) تَوَدَّعَ 5 see 1. دَعَةٌ Ease; repose; freedom from trouble or inconvenience, and toil or fatigue; tranquillity; syn. خَفْضٌ (S. Mgh, K, TA) and رَاحَةٌ (Mgh, Msb, TA) and سُكُونٌ; (TA;) and ampleness of circumstances (سَعَةٌ) in life: (K:) or دَعَةٌ is syn. with راحة and سكون; but خَفْضٌ signifies “ ampleness of the circumstances ” (سَعَهٌّ) of life, and “ plentifulness and pleasantness ” thereof: [see an ex. of both, voce خَفْضٌ]. (El-Marzookee and MF, art. خفض.) ___ See 1 and 3. مَوْدُوعٌ : see وَدَاعٌ, and see a verse cited voce مَصْدَق. مُسْتَوْدَعٌ A depository: see a verse cited voce ظِلٌّ. مِيدَعٌ and مِيدَعَةٌ A garment, or piece of cloth, used as a repository for clothes. (TA.) وَادَعَهُ 3 , inf. n. مُوَادَعَةٌ, and subst. وِدَاعٌ, He made peace or reconciled himself, with him: (Msb:) [the inf. n.] مُوَادَعَةٌ is syn. with مُصَالَحَةٌ because it is مُتَارَكَةٌ [a mutual leaving, or leaving unmolested]. (Mgh.) === مُوَادَعَةٌ is also syn. with تَرْكٌ; as also 🡻دَعَةٌ: (TA:) so that وَادَعَهُ signifies He left him: but more correctly, he left him, being left by him; like تَارَكَهُ, and خَالَاهُ; and this is the primary meaning. وَدَاعٌ [Gravity, steadiness:] i. q. سَكِينَةٌ, [like 🡻مَوْدُوعٌ,] as also وَقَارٌ. (S, L, in art. سكن.) === And Valediction. (S, Msb.) وَدَعَةٌ A cowry; Cypræa: see an ex. cited voce سَمَّ. وَدُعَ 1 , (S, K,) inf. n. وَدْعٌ and دَعَةٌ, (TA,) He (a man, S,) or it, (a thing, TA,) became still, quiet, or at rest; (S, K, TA;) as also 🡻اِتَّدَعَ, (K, TA,) [quasi-] inf. n. تُدْعَةٌ and تُدَعَةٌ. (TA.) You say to a man, 🡻اِنَّدَعْ and 🡻تَوَدَّعْ meaning Be thou grave, staid, steady, sedate, or calm. (TA.) See also 1; and art. وذر. === وَدُعَ and وَدَعَ, inf. n. وَدَاعَةٌ, He was, or became, in a state of ease, and ampleness of the means or circumstances of life. (Msb.) === دَعْ: see an ex. voce آذَى: it may be rendered, in different cases, Leave thou, or let alone, or say nothing of: see بَلْهَ. === دَعْنِى مِنْ كَذَا Let me alone and cease from such a thing: and exempt thou me, or excuse me, from such a thing. === دَعْ عَنْكَ كَذَا Dismiss thou from thee such a thing. See خُذْ عَنْكَ. === دَعْ مَا يَرِيبُكَ إِلَى لَا يَرِيبُكَ: see art. ريب. === دَعْ مَا زَيْدٌ: see سِىٌّ in art. سوى. === وَدَعَ used as a pret.: see an ex. voce زَائِلَةٌ, in art. زول. وَدِيعَةٌ A thing committed to the trust and care of a person; a trust; a deposit. (Mgh, Msb.) See 10. وَدَّعَهُ 2 , (MA,) inf. n. تَوْديعٌ, (PS,) He bade farewell to him. (MA, PS.)
